title: Cross-chain liquidation sends unmapped collateral-market identifiers without validation
reason: Configuration-dependent and largely subsumed by already-tracked cross-chain liquidation finalization/revert issues (F-006/F-023); not retained as a distinct root-cause finding.

title: Borrower reward distribution can hard-revert on zero borrow index
reason: Requires `borrowIndex == 0` on a listed market, which is not realistic for standard initialized lTokens; treated as overly speculative.

title: Cross-chain liquidation success uses incorrect srcEid for borrow lookup
reason: Already captured by F-006 (lookup parameter mismatch and token-identity inconsistency in liquidation finalization).

title: LEND distribution uses mixed borrow indices causing calculation inconsistency
reason: No distinct new exploit path beyond existing cross-chain borrow-index mismatch accounting issues; insufficient standalone protocol-harm evidence.

title: Cross-chain liquidation uses seize amount as synthetic borrow in health check
Liquidation failure handler transfers tokens without balance verification
reason: Weaker restatement of already tracked escrow/refund inconsistency (F-019); `safeTransfer` itself reverts on failure.

title: Cross-chain borrow request allows borrowing without entering destination market
reason: Not a protocol bug in this model; borrow market membership is not the missing enforcement root-cause for the reported harms.

title: Supply function uses pre-mint exchange rate after token transfer
